replication_study.hypothesis: Among preschool teachers, higher weekend relaxation (measured at Time-2 with the Recovery Experiences Questionnaire) will be associated with higher joviality at the end of the following work-week (Time-3 PANAS joviality) after adjusting for baseline joviality (Time-1) and standard demographic and weekend-experience covariates.

data_plan.source_type: Online self-report survey (Qualtrics panels of preschool teachers)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: 3 survey occasions per participant (T1 Friday, T2 Sunday, T3 following Friday); Waves labelled 1–31 in variable “Wave”.
data_plan.sample_size: N = 339 respondents who completed all three occasions ( get_dataset_shape shows 339 rows ).
data_plan.access_details: Openly provided in replication_data folder; no additional restrictions.

qualification.explanation: The dataset contains the same constructs collected with the same instruments (PANAS affect scales, Recovery Experiences Questionnaire, daily hassles index) across the identical three-time-point design, thereby allowing a direct test of the original claim on an independent sample.
qualification.similarity_to_original:
• The key variables req_relax (weekend relaxation) and T3_panas_joviality (end-week joviality) appear in the processed file (k17_processed_data.csv columns listed via get_dataset_columns).
• The original claim (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json) used the same scales and design (3 time points, identical covariates).
qualification.deviation_from_original:
• Larger and more recent sample (339 vs original 229; see get_dataset_shape vs post_registration.json sample_size).
• Data collected online in 2021 (age recoded as 2021-birthyear in k17_data_prep.R) whereas original study used 2009 paper-and-pencil surveys (post_registration.json data.source).

data_plan.notes: All variables already aggregated/recoded in the processed file. Missing values remain as NA; lm will drop listwise. Demographic variables: gender coded 1=male 2=female, children dummy recoded inside preprocessing script.

steps: Standardise continuous predictors (age, work_hours, work_days, baseline joviality, req_* variables, hassles) using scale().
Estimate three nested OLS models as in k17_analysis.R: Model_1 (demographics only), Model_2 (+baseline joviality), Model_3 (+weekend experience predictors).
Extract the coefficient, standard error, t & p value for req_relax in Model_3 (focal test).
Assess replication success according to inference criteria.
planned_method.independent_variables: req_relax (mean of four relaxation items, Time-2).
planned_method.control_variables: age, gender, children, work_hours, work_days, T1_panas_joviality, req_control, req_mastery, req_detach, hassles.
planned_method.tools_software: R ≥4.2 with base ‘stats’; optional ‘tidyverse’ for data handling.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Standardised regression coefficient (β) for req_relax in Model_3.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test on the regression coefficient (one-tailed, H1: β > 0).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion performed automatically by lm after scaling.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replication successful if β_relaxation is positive and p_one-tailed < 0.05.

files.k17_data_prep.R: Creates k17_processed_data.csv from raw Qualtrics exports (T1, T2, T3). Handles variable renaming, aggregation (REQ scales, PANAS dimensions, hassles sum) and basic cleaning.
files.k17_analysis.R: Runs hierarchical regression models (Model_1–Model_3) on score_all (identical to k17_processed_data) and prints focal results.
files.k17_analysis_preregistration.R: Variant scripted for prereg report; identical statistical core.
files.k17_data_dictionary.csv: Variable names, labels and scale descriptions to facilitate interpretation.
files.k17_data_prep__py.py: Utility Python translation of the R prep; not required if R script runs.

codebase.notes: All scripts assume working directory with data files. Paths in k17_data_prep.R need adjustment to '/app/data/Raw data/…' when executed inside container.

analysis.instructions: Inside the container: (1) setwd('/app/data/replication_data'); (2) source('k17_analysis.R'); the script loads k17_processed_data.csv and prints summary(Model_3).
analysis.comparison_metrics: 1) Sign of β for req_relax; 2) One-tailed p-value; 3) Magnitude of β relative to original (0.15).
